prerequisites: Internet access to reach the public RKI Covid API at https://api.corona-zahlen.org (the data system the connector reads from).
A start_date value (the earliest date from which to pull the COVID-19 time-series records).
No account, API key, or token is required: the RKI Covid API is a free, public REST API maintained by Marlon Lueckert and sourced from the Robert Koch-Institut.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The RKI Covid API (api.corona-zahlen.org) is a free, public REST API and requires no API key, token, or credentials. The connector defines no secret fields, so no authentication is configured.

setupSteps.title: Confirm API reachability
setupSteps.body: Verify that the host running pm can reach the public endpoint at https://api.corona-zahlen.org over HTTPS. No credentials are needed; the API responds with JSON and requires only outbound internet access.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Configure the required start_date field with the earliest date from which to retrieve COVID-19 history. The connector uses this to bound the time-series it pulls from the API's history endpoints (for example /germany/history/cases), which return records back to the requested point in time.

setupSteps.title: Run the sync
setupSteps.body: Start the source. With start_date set and no secrets to supply, the connector queries the RKI Covid API and emits the COVID-19 time-series records (cases, deaths, recoveries, incidence, and related metrics) as JSON.

sources.title: Robert Koch-Institut COVID-19 API (official docs, Marlon Lueckert)
RKI COVID-19 API - Germany endpoints (history / date filtering)
